The Atlantic School · quality over quantity Envelopes · 05
Envelope 01
06 / 15
01
Read aloud, please

"What's one thing the industry has gotten very wrong about technology for advisors and their teams?"

Lead: Kelly · Echo: Matt, Alex, Mike · 60 sec each

The cringy but honest answer

It was built to solve a problem, but, it's never lived inside YOUR practice.

Tools that work in a demo likely break in your workflow. Whyyyyyyyyyy? Because nobody who built them has ever lived a Tuesday morning at your desk.

  • Promise the world, deliver 10% of the usage
  • "Help Centre" = articles no body reads, a video library nobody watches, bots with no context for YOUR business
  • Ghosted instead of supported
  • Nickel-and-dimed across a tech stack that doesn't do what it's supposed to

The fix
Context is king. Get clear on the actual problem you're trying to solve first. The right tool only shows up once you know what you're really solving for.

Envelope 02
07 / 15
02
Read aloud, please

"I have forty systems. None of them talk to each other. Where do I even start?"

Lead: Kelly · Echo: Alex

The ugg-worthy honest answer

Start with the problem you're solving. Always.

People first, then process. Software is last, not first. Map the team. Map the workflows. Then, and only then, buy or replace tech.

  • Get the team participating in the decisions: roles, accountabilities, who owns what
  • Solve the right problem, and the whole problem
  • Share your definitions: what does ‘integrate’ actually mean to you?
01

People

Roles, capacity, who's drowning.

02

Process

Workflows on paper, then handoffs.

03

Tech

Mapped to each problem you are solving.

Takeaway · the order is the answer.
Envelope 03
08 / 15
03
Read aloud, please

"We use less than 10% of every tool I pay for. How do I fix that?"

Lead: Matt · Echo: Michael + Alex

The honest answer

Lack of planning is the real failure point.

A tool without a plan is just expensive software. When nobody owns the 'why' and the 'how', your people check out, fall back on old habits, and the same failure repeats. The fix for that gap lives in the planning.

  • Your people are disengaged
  • Nobody is clear on the 'why' or the 'how'
  • Mindset stuck on repeat, which is how failure compounds
Takeaway · plan first, and the platform follows.
Envelope 04
09 / 15
04
Read aloud, please

"How do I know I'm offside before an auditor or regulator tells me I am?"

Lead: Kelly · Echo: Matt

The take-a-deep-breath answer

Every practice is offside somewhere.

One license in one jurisdiction still answers to a minimum of 25 stakeholders. The norm is misalignment. The exception is clarity. We get you the exception.

Laws
Regulators
MGAs
CRA
Carriers
Designations
CE
PIPEDA
CASL
… +16 more
Takeaway · a clear risk profile = lighter mental load. Period.
Envelope 05
10 / 15
05
Read aloud, please

"I'm thinking about retirement. What should I do about technology now?"

Lead: Matt · Echo: Alex, Mike · Also for the firm with a non-tech-savvy legacy advisor

The answer you've been avoiding

Your tech stack is part of your succession plan.

Buyers don't want to buy paper and chaos. A clean stack, a documented workflow, and client intelligence make your book more valuable and the transfer smoother.

Year −5 to −3

Clean stack

Consolidate, document, train the team.

Year −3 to −1

Organized data

Clean records, clear client intelligence.

Year 0

Calm transition + max return

Clients stay. Team stays. Value stays.

Takeaway · the business has to run without you before it can be sold.
Envelope 06
11 / 15
06
Read aloud, please

"How do I bring AI into my practice without creating more risk?"

Lead: Alex · Echo: Mike, Kelly

The sleep-at-night answer

Use AI that already knows the rules.

Generic AI doesn't know what KYC, AML, PIPEDA or CASL mean for you. Compliant-by-design AI does.

  • No meeting bots on calls (Continuum)
  • End-to-end encryption and audit-trail logging
  • Compliance-aware planning outputs (Friedmann.ai)
  • Etc. AI guardrails wrapped around your practice profile
Takeaway · AI for advisors is not AI for everyone. Demand the difference.
Envelope 07
12 / 15
07
Read aloud, please

"What does good actually look like in 2026?"

Lead: All four · Echo: one sentence each · Bring it home

Kelly

"You know exactly where your practice stands, and exactly what the next move is."

Risk profileCompliance processGrowth opportunitiesGaps
Matt

"Your team uses the tools you pay for. All of them. Without you nagging."

AdoptionTeam trainingWorkflow fitAccountability
Alex

"Every client interaction makes the next one sharper, without you opening a single spreadsheet."

Client intelligenceData captureAutomationInsight
Mike

"Plans your clients actually understand, built in minutes, defensible by design."

ClaritySpeedDefensibleClient trust
Takeaway · "good" is calm. Calm is buildable.
